solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1170F - Wheels
It is obvious that it is always better to try to equalize the subsegment of the sorted array. So let's sort the array and iterate over all $$$n-m+1$$$ of its subsegments.
Let $$$sum(l, r) = \sum\limits_{j=l}^{r} a_j$$$.
For the segment $$$[i; i + m - 1]$$$ we have to find the minimum number of moves needed to equalize this segment. In the best case (if $$$k=\infty$$$) it can be proven that the better way to equalize all elements is to move them to the middle element of the segment (or if $$$m$$$ is even then we can choose either the left middle or the right middle element). How to calculate answer for this case? Let $$$idx$$$ be the middle of the segment (in other words, $$$i + \lfloor\frac{m}{2}\rfloor$$$), $$$val$$$ be the middle element (in other words, $$$a_{idx}$$$) and $$$cnt$$$ be $$$idx - i + 1$$$. Then the number of moves we need is $$$res = (cnt \cdot val - sum(i, idx)) + (sum(idx + 1, i + m - 1) - (m - cnt) \cdot val)$$$. This formula gives the number of operations needed to equalize the whole segment to the value $$$val$$$. The left part of the formula increases the part to the left of $$$val$$$ and the rightmost part decreases the part to the right of $$$val$$$.
This is almost the complete solution! What should we do with $$$k$$$? Let's find the rightmost position $$$idx$$$ that belongs to the segment and we can increase all elements in segment $$$[i; idx]$$$ to the value $$$a_{idx}$$$. We can do it using binary search and prefix sums (or two pointers). Then consider two cases:
$$$idx  \lt  i + \lfloor\frac{m}{2}\rfloor$$$;
$$$idx \ge i + \lfloor\frac{m}{2}\rfloor$$$.
In the first case $$$idx$$$ does not change, $$$cnt = i + idx - 1$$$ and $$$val = a_{idx} + \lfloor\frac{k - (cnt \cdot a_{idx} - sum(i, idx))}{cnt}\rfloor$$$. Then we can substitute these variables to the formula calculating $$$res$$$ and update the answer with the result. What does this scary formula ($$$\lfloor\frac{k - (cnt \cdot a_{idx} - sum(i, idx))}{cnt}\rfloor$$$) mean? It means that we can increase all values on the segment $$$[i; idx]$$$ to the value $$$a_{idx}$$$ but there are some increasing opreations remain and we should use them. And because $$$idx$$$ is the rightmost such position, it means that we cannot increase all values to $$$a_{idx + 1}$$$ but we can increase the whole segment $$$[i; idx]$$$ by some value possible greater than $$$a_{idx}$$$ (and it will not increase the result for this segment).
In the second case $$$idx = i + \lfloor\frac{m}{2}\rfloor$$$, $$$cnt = idx - i + 1$$$ and $$$val = a_{idx}$$$, and we can substitute these variables into the formula calculating $$$res$$$ and update the answer with the result.
